title: dianthus carthusianorum subsp. latifolius
intro: Dianthus carthusianorum subsp. latifolius, also known as the Fragrant Pink, is a subspecies of the Dianthus carthusianorum plant, a type of flowering plant in the carnation family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its essential oil and flavonoids. The Fragrant Pink is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of menopause and PMS. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
